zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Courtenay於大約1334年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特時，她的父親Hugh Courtenay III32歲，母親Margaret de Bohun24歲。她於大約1341年7月24日與Sir John de Vere結婚。她去世於1395年8月7日享年62歲，埋葬在英國戴文艾可謝特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Courtenay于大约1334年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特时，她的父亲Hugh Courtenay III32岁，母亲Margaret de Bohun24岁。她于大约1341年7月24日与Sir John de Vere结婚。她去世于1395年8月7日享年62岁，埋葬在英国戴文艾可謝特。

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Courtenay was born about 1334, in Exeter, Devon, England, her father, Hugh Courtenay III, was 32 and her mother, Margaret de Bohun, was 24. She married Sir John de Vere about 24 July 1341. She died on 7 August 1395, at the age of 62, and was buried in Exeter, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Courtenay est née environ 1334, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Courtenay III, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Bohun, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Vere environ 24 juillet 1341. Elle est décédée le 7 août 1395, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Courtenay um 1334 in Exeter, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Courtenay III 32 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Bohun 24. Sie hat um 24. Juli 1341 Sir John de Vere geheiratet. Sie starb am 7. August 1395 im Alter von 62 Jahren und wurde in Exeter, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Courtenay è nata il circa 1334 nel Exeter, Devon, England, sua padre, Hugh Courtenay III, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Bohun, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir John de Vere circa 24 luglio 1341. Morì il 7 agosto 1395, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Exeter, Devon, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Courtenay(이)가 대략 1334년 영국 데폰 세터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Courtenay III(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Bohun(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1341년 7월 24일 Sir John de Vere(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1395년 8월 7일 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 세터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Courtenay nasceu aproximadamente 1334, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Courtenay III, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Bohun, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Vere aproximadamente 24 de julho de 1341. Ela faleceu em 7 de agosto de 1395, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Courtenay родилась около 1334 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Courtenay III было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Bohun, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Vere около 24 июля 1341 г. Она умерла 7 августа 1395 г. в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности эксетир, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Courtenay nació aproximadamente 1334, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Courtenay III, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Margaret de Bohun, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Sir John de Vere aproximadamente 24 de julio de 1341. Murió el 7 de agosto de 1395, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.
